_meta.description: Machine-readable product rules for GEM India QA oracle. Derived from authoritative sources, NOT from implementation code.
sources: CLAUDE.md — Critical Rules and Architecture sections
research-hub/PROJECT_HANDOFF.md — Tech stack and design tokens
qa/TEST_ORACLE.md — Oracle hierarchy and product rules

invariants.name: event_id_filtering
invariants.rule: Every database query MUST filter by eventId. No cross-event data leakage is permitted.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Never skip eventId filtering in any database query. Every query must scope to the active event.'
invariants.negative_test: A query without eventId filter MUST return an error or empty result, never data from another event.

invariants.name: zod_input_validation
invariants.rule: Every API route MUST validate input with Zod before processing.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Every API route validates input with Zod before processing.'
invariants.negative_test: An API request with malformed/missing required fields MUST be rejected with a validation error before any database operation.

invariants.name: audit_trail
invariants.rule: Every mutation to travel, accommodation, or transport MUST write to the audit log. Audit log is append-only.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Every mutation to travel/accommodation/transport writes to the audit log.'
invariants.negative_test: A mutation to travel/accommodation/transport that does NOT produce an audit log entry is a critical failure.

invariants.name: notification_idempotency
invariants.rule: Every notification send MUST check the idempotency key in Redis before sending. Duplicate sends MUST NOT dispatch a second notification.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Every notification send checks the idempotency key in Redis before sending.'
invariants.negative_test: Sending the same notification twice with the same idempotency key MUST result in exactly one delivery.
invariants.note: Exact response behavior for duplicate send attempts is module-specific. NEEDS_HUMAN_DECISION for whether duplicate returns success-with-noop or explicit duplicate-rejection.

invariants.name: phone_e164_normalization
invariants.rule: Phone numbers MUST be normalized to E.164 format on input using libphonenumber-js.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Phone numbers are normalized to E.164 on input using libphonenumber-js.'
invariants.negative_test: A phone number stored without E.164 normalization is a failure. Invalid phone numbers MUST be rejected.
invariants.note: Default region for normalization (IN vs none) NEEDS_HUMAN_DECISION if not specified per-form.

invariants.name: timestamp_utc_ist
invariants.rule: Timestamps MUST be stored in UTC. Timestamps MUST be displayed in IST (Asia/Kolkata) using date-fns-tz.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'Timestamps stored in UTC, displayed in IST using date-fns-tz.'
invariants.negative_test: A timestamp stored in non-UTC format, or displayed in non-IST timezone, is a failure.

invariants.name: file_upload_limit
invariants.negative_test: A file larger than 20MB MUST be rejected before upload completes.

invariants.name: role_enforcement
invariants.rule: Permissions checked with has() from @clerk/nextjs. UI elements hidden for roles without access. Write buttons disabled (not hidden) for Read-only role.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: Roles table + 'Hide UI elements the role cannot access. Disable (don't hide) write buttons for Read-only.'
invariants.negative_test: A role accessing a route or action it should not have access to is a critical failure. Read-only seeing enabled write buttons is a failure.

invariants.name: notification_routing
invariants.rule: All notifications MUST go through lib/notifications/. Never call Resend or Evolution API directly from a page or API route.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'All notifications go through lib/notifications/. Never call Resend or Evolution API directly.'
invariants.negative_test: A direct call to Resend or Evolution API from outside lib/notifications/ is a critical architectural violation.

invariants.name: cascade_events
invariants.rule: When travel/accommodation/session data changes, an Inngest event MUST be emitted. Never manually trigger downstream updates.

cascade_definitions.conference/travel.updated: flag accommodation + recalculate transport + notify delegate
cascade_definitions.conference/session.updated: notify affected faculty with revised responsibilities

invariants.negative_test: A travel/accommodation/session mutation that does NOT emit the corresponding Inngest event is a failure.

invariants.name: red_flag_lifecycle
invariants.rule: Red flags follow a 3-state lifecycle: unreviewed (red) -> reviewed (yellow) -> resolved (cleared). Flag records store flag_type, flag_detail, flag_status, flag_created_at, reviewed_by, resolved_by.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: Red Flags Table section; research-hub/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md: UX Decision 2
invariants.negative_test: A flag that skips states (e.g., unreviewed directly to resolved) or lacks required fields is a failure.

invariants.name: no_secrets_in_code
invariants.rule: Never hardcode API keys, tokens, secrets, or credentials. Use environment variables.
invariants.source: AGENTS.md: 'NEVER hardcode API keys, tokens, secrets, or credentials.'
invariants.negative_test: Any hardcoded secret, API key, or credential in source code is a critical security failure.

locked_design_decisions.decision: WhatsApp via Evolution API Docker sidecar
Dashboard home: event selector -> metrics -> quick actions
Red-flag cascade: 3-state lifecycle with badge showing WHAT changed and WHEN changed
Scientific Program: attendee auto-switches card list (<768px) to grid (>=768px); admin always grid with horizontal scroll
